alternative_parents.description: Aromatic heterocyclic compounds containing a purine moiety, which is formed a pyrimidine-ring ring fused to an imidazole ring.
alternative_parents.name: Purines and purine derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Aromatic compounds containing a halogen atom linked to a pyrimidine ring. Pyrimidine is a 6-membered ring consisting of four carbon atoms and two nitrogen centers at the 1- and 3- ring positions.
alternative_parents.name: Halopyrimidines

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an amino group attached to a pyrimidine ring. Pyrimidine is a 6-membered ring consisting of four carbon atoms and two nitrogen centers at the 1- and 3- ring positions.
alternative_parents.name: Aminopyrimidines and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Heterocyclic compounds containing an imidazole ring substituted at position 1.
alternative_parents.name: N-substituted imidazoles

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing the acyl chloride functional group.
alternative_parents.name: Aryl chlorides

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an aromatic ring where a carbon atom is linked to an hetero atom.
alternative_parents.name: Heteroaromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds comprising the primary alcohol functional group, with the general structure RCOH (R=alkyl, aryl).
alternative_parents.name: Primary alcohols

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a chemical bond between a carbon atom and a chlorine atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organochlorides

alternative_parents.description: Organic neutral compounds having formal unit electrical charges of opposite sign.
alternative_parents.name: Organic zwitterions

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a chlorine ion.
alternative_parents.name: Organic chloride salts

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds formally derived from ammonia by replacing one, two or three hydrogen atoms by hydrocarbyl groups, and having the general structures RNH2 (primary amines), R2NH (secondary amines), R3N (tertiary amines).
alternative_parents.name: Amines

class.description: Analogues of nucleosides and nucleotides. These include phosphonated nucleosides, C-glycosylated nucleoside bases, analogues where the sugar unit is a pyranose, and carbocyclic nucleosides, among others.
class.name: Nucleoside and nucleotide analogues

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as 1,3-substituted cyclopentyl purine nucleosides. These are nucleoside analogues with a structure that consists of a cyclobutane that is substituted a the 1-position with a hydroxyl group and at the 3-position with either a purine base.

direct_parent.description: Nucleoside analogues with a structure that consists of a cyclobutane that is substituted a the 1-position with a hydroxyl group and at the 3-position with either a purine base.
direct_parent.name: 1,3-substituted cyclopentyl purine nucleosides

intermediate_nodes.description: Nucleoside analogues with a structure that consists of a cyclobutane that is substituted a the 1-position with a hydroxyl group and at the 3-position with either a purine or pyrimidine base.
intermediate_nodes.name: 1,3-substituted cyclopentyl nucleosides

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

subclass.description: Nucleoside analogues with a structure that consists of a cyclobutane that is substituted a the 1-position with a hydroxyl group and at the 2- or the 3- position with either a purine or pyrimidine base.
subclass.name: Cyclopentyl nucleosides

superclass.description: Compounds containing a nucleobase linked to a ribose or deoxyribose sugar via a beta-glycosidic linkage. The ribose or deoxyribose moiety bears at least a phosphate group in case of nucleotides.
superclass.name: Nucleosides, nucleotides, and analogues
